text.tspan: adding/removing one server shouldn't move every key
FORMAL: modulo hashing moves O(K) keys on resize.
consistent hashing moves O(K/N) keys on resize.
For K=1B keys and N=100 servers, that's 10M moves instead of ~1B u2014
the difference between 'do it during business hours' and 'maintenance window'.
u2192 alice MOVED. So did about 80% of all keys.
u2192 every key needs re-checking. Cache invalidation storm.
BEFORE (N=4): key 'alice' -> hash=12345 -> next clockwise server on ring
AFTER (N=5): adding server E changes ownership ONLY in E's arc
u2192 alice MAY have moved (if E sits between alice and her old server)
u2192 but on average only K/N keys move when adding 1 server
u2192 ~80% of keys keep their current ownership
u2192 cache hit rate barely dips; rebalance is gradual
